verbGlossary.TRACKED AS: {source} attribute is represented by {target} (e.g., project status tracked as open, closed, on hold, or void)
verbGlossary.ADVANCES THROUGH: {source} phase attribute progresses by moving through {target} steps (e.g., current phase advances through workflow steps from preconstruction to closeout)
verbGlossary.REFLECTED BY: {source} completion attribute is indicated by {target} (e.g., percent complete reflected by in-progress status on active items)
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source} attribute mandates the use of {target} pattern (e.g., fast-track enforces parallel review to maintain overlapping design and construction)
verbGlossary.CONFIGURES: {source} delivery, contract, or owner attribute determines the structure of {target} pattern or approval chain (e.g., delivery method configures sequential approval and approval chain depth)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} schedule attribute activates {target} when deadlines are missed (e.g., planned completion date triggers overdue status on incomplete items)
verbGlossary.ANCHORS: {source} date attribute sets the reference point for {target} due date (e.g., planned completion date anchors due dates for submittals and closeout items)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED AGAINST: {source} performance attribute is compared to {target} efficiency metric (e.g., submittal approval rate benchmarked against first-pass approval rate)
verbGlossary.CORRELATED WITH: {source} rate metric shows statistical relationship with {target} timing metric (e.g., change order rate correlated with average approval cycle time)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTS: {source} subdivides {target} metric for comparative analysis (e.g., project type segments overdue rate, total value band segments escalation rate)
verbGlossary.SCALES: {source} complexity attribute increases the size of {target} distribution list (e.g., number of subcontractors scales distribution list size)
verbGlossary.MULTIPLIES: {source} phasing attribute creates additional instances of {target} element (e.g., phased construction multiplies workflow steps for each construction phase)
verbGlossary.ACTIVATES: {source} attribute triggers {target} pattern for specialty work (e.g., specialty systems activate inspect-and-disposition workflows for commissioning)
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source} certification attribute requires {target} as a compliance prerequisite (e.g., LEED certification mandates review-and-respond workflows for commissioning documentation)
verbGlossary.DRIVEN BY: {source} outcome is directly influenced by the complexity and volume of {target} processes -- more {target} steps means higher {source}
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} outcome is quantified through {target} metric, which provides a concrete measurement of the abstract concept
verbGlossary.SLOWED BY: {source} outcome is degraded by {target} process design that adds sequential handoffs and wait states
